titleStmt.title: SII 27.335: original edition by M. D. Sampath, H. K. Narasimhamurthy, Madhav N. Katti and B. R. Gopal
No. 335. (A.R. No. 499 of 1928-29.) NĪLĀVARA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.

summary.p: Right side of the slab (No. 8) set up in thee prākāra of Mahishāsuramardini temple.

This record assignable on palaeographical grounds to the 15th century is dated the cyclic year Vikrama, Chaitra ba. 30, Friday, solar eclipse. The given details correspond regularly to 1400 A.D., March 26, when the month was Adhika Chaitra.

The inscription records the renovation of the temple of Durggādēvī by the sixteen of the village headed by Bayiraṇṇa and registers a gift of specified quantities of rice for the daily worship of and offerings to the goddess for the merit of mahāpradhāna Īrugappa-daṇṇāyaka.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī vikramasaṁvatsa
rada caitra ba 30 śu
sūryya grahaṇa da
li śrīmanmahāpradhāna
Irugappa daṁṇāyaka
rige bayiraṁṇagaḷu
māḍida dharṁma Ā grāma
hadināṟu muṁtāgi
durgādēvige jīrnnōddhāra
māḍi dinaṁ prati nayivē
dyakke naḍava Akki hāni 5 kaṁ
rāmage Akki hāni
gariṁge Akki hāni 2 kaṁ
damaged
Digital edition of SII 27.335 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
